item-045.title: NC State University — Dept. of Forestry & Environmental Resources
item-045.org: Raleigh, NC · MS Forestry / Natural Resources — thesis track
item-045.body: NC State remains Erin's single strongest institutional match. Dr. Jodi Forrester's lab focuses on forest recovery from disturbance events in western NC — directly overlapping with Erin's Pigeon River capstone. Even if the specific May 2026 position is filled, Fall 2027 positions in the same lab o...

item-048.title: University of Montana — MS Wildlife Biology
item-048.deadline: January 15, 2027 (unless faculty specify otherwise for RA positions)
item-048.body: UM's Wildlife Biology MS program receives ~100 applications/year and only accepts students with confirmed funding via RA or TA. Strong mammalogy and population ecology faculty. WICHE discount available for some states (check eligibility). TA packages include full tuition waiver. January 15 priori...

item-050.title: Colorado State University — Ecosystem Science & Sustainability / GDPE
item-050.org: Fort Collins, CO · MS or PhD — multiple departments
item-050.body: CSU maintains 40–50 active graduate assistantships per semester across Ecosystem Science, Ecology, and related departments. The Landscape Modeling Lab (wetland remote sensing, Prairie Pothole Region) and Water, Environment & Society Group are both strong Erin fits. For Fall 2027 entry, Erin shoul...

item-049.title: Mississippi State University — Wildlife, Fisheries & Aquaculture
item-049.org: Starkville, MS · MS Wildlife or Fisheries
item-049.body: MSU's WFA department only accepts graduate students on funded projects. Contact faculty directly before applying — the department explicitly states that blind applications are less effective. Typical MS stipend $18,000–$22,000/yr + tuition waiver. The Forest & Wildlife Research Center (FWRC) and ...

item-030.title: Biological Science Technician (GS-0404)
item-030.org: US Fish & Wildlife Service · Southeast Region (Region 4) NWR offices
item-030.body: USFWS Biological Science Technician positions (GS-0404 series) involve wildlife surveys, wetland management, habitat assessments, and species monitoring on National Wildlife Refuges. Region 4 covers NC, SC, GA, FL, AL, MS, TN, KY, AR — the region where Erin has direct field experience. Entry-leve...

item-033.title: Wildlife Biologist I / Wildlife Technician
item-033.body: NC Wildlife Resources Commission hires wildlife biologists and technicians for population monitoring, habitat assessment, GIS analysis, and species management across NC. Strong match for Erin's NRM 479 (Mammalogy), NRM 330 (Wildlife Management), and GIS coursework. NCWRC also conducts significant...

item-039.title: Environmental Conservation Corps — Restoration Crew & Community Restoration Members
item-039.org: The Sustainability Institute · Charleston / Lowcountry, SC & Bucksport/Conway, SC
item-039.body: Currently recruiting for the 2026 program year. Two positions available. Restoration Crew Members restore wildlife habitat and ecosystems across the Lowcountry: salt marshes, oyster reefs, rain gardens, native planting, water quality monitoring, erosion control, trails, and boardwalks. Community ...

item-038.title: BLM Wetlands & Wildlife Technician — Individual Placement
item-038.org: Southwest Conservation Corps / Bureau of Land Management · San Luis Valley Field Office · Monte Vista, CO
item-038.deadline: Active — posted May 2026; apply as soon as possible
item-038.body: This position is the closest match to Erin's full skill set of any AmeriCorps position found. It was with the BLM San Luis Valley Field Office, which manages over 500,000 acres including 22,000+ acres of some of the most ecologically significant wetland and wildlife habitats in Colorado. The IP w...
item-038.desc: This position is the closest match to Erin's full skill set of any AmeriCorps position found. It was with the BLM San Luis Valley Field Office, which manages over 500,000 acres including 22,000+ acres of some of the most ecologically significant wetland and wildlife habitats in Colorado. The IP worked directly with BLM biologists on habitat assessments, wildlife surveys, water quality monitoring, GIS data analysis, invasive species management, and wetland well monitoring. This exact type of position recurs annually — watch for the 2026–27 version.

item-080.title: Conservation Corps Minnesota & Iowa — Individual Placements
item-080.deadline: Rolling — new positions posted continuously at conservationcorps.org/join
item-080.body: Conservation Corps MN & IA places AmeriCorps members with Soil and Water Conservation Districts and natural resource agencies. Wetland inventories, water quality monitoring, GIS mapping, species surveys. Full-year IPs and summer positions. New positions roll continuously.
item-080.desc: Conservation Corps Minnesota & Iowa places AmeriCorps members with Soil and Water Conservation Districts and natural resource agencies. Summer soil and water positions include wetland and watercourse inventories, field surveys, groundwater monitoring, and educational programs. Full-year IPs include GIS mapping, species inventory, invasive species management, and water quality monitoring at agencies like MN DNR, USFS, and nonprofit partners. Strong match for Erin’s GIS, water quality, and wildlife inventory skills. New positions post continuously at conservationcorps.org/join.

item-069.title: Biological Science Technician IP — Cherokee & Pisgah National Forests
item-069.org: Southeast Conservation Corps / US Forest Service · Eastern TN / Western NC
item-069.body: Southeast Conservation Corps regularly places AmeriCorps members with US Forest Service fisheries and wildlife teams in Cherokee National Forest (TN) and Pisgah National Forest (NC) — the exact landscape of Erin's Hurricane Helene research. Duties include aquatic resource monitoring, stream resto...
item-069.desc: Southeast Conservation Corps regularly places AmeriCorps members with US Forest Service fisheries and wildlife teams in Cherokee National Forest (TN) and Pisgah National Forest (NC) — the exact landscape of Erin's Hurricane Helene research. Duties include aquatic resource monitoring, stream restoration, electrofishing, macroinvertebrate surveys, and freshwater snorkeling. This is a direct follow-on to her GEOL 423 (Contaminated Rivers) and ENVH 310 (Water Quality) coursework in the same geography.

item-072.title: USDA Forest Service Pathways Recent Graduate Program
item-072.org: USDA Forest Service · Multiple offices, SE and Mountain West
item-072.body: The USDA Forest Service Pathways Recent Graduate Program places graduates within the past 2 years in 1-year developmental positions that convert to permanent federal employment. Biological science, hydrology, GIS, and natural resources positions are available. Erin graduated May 2025 — eligible t...
item-072.desc: The USDA Forest Service Pathways Recent Graduate Program places graduates within the past 2 years in 1-year developmental positions that convert to permanent federal employment. Biological science, hydrology, GIS, and natural resources positions are available. Erin graduated May 2025 — eligible through May 2027. This is a direct federal career pathway without the competitive hurdle of the standard hiring process. Search USAJobs for "Pathways Recent Graduate USDA Forest Service" filtered by biological science or natural resources series.

item-058.title: Entry-Level Environmental Scientist — SE Region
item-058.org: Tetra Tech / AECOM / Geosyntec · Raleigh NC, Asheville NC, Charleston SC, Atlanta GA
item-058.body: Tetra Tech has an active Entry-Level Environmental Scientist posting for southeastern US (Aiken, SC area) focused on environmental permitting and regulatory compliance, with wetland delineation experience as a plus. AECOM is recruiting for an entry-level Environmental Scientist in GA, AL, FL, and...
item-058.desc: Tetra Tech has an active Entry-Level Environmental Scientist posting for southeastern US (Aiken, SC area) focused on environmental permitting and regulatory compliance, with wetland delineation experience as a plus. AECOM is recruiting for an entry-level Environmental Scientist in GA, AL, FL, and SC starting spring/summer 2026 — an Aug/Sep start for a qualified candidate is feasible. Geosyntec has openings in Asheville, NC specifically. All hire on rolling basis throughout the year.

item-094.title: Colorado State University — Funded MS, Mesocarnivore Ecology (Gerber Lab)
item-094.org: Fort Collins, CO · Dept. of Fish, Wildlife, & Conservation Biology
item-094.deadline: Mar 1, 2026 priority (passed) — next cycle ~Mar 2027 (Fall 2027 start); may inquire now
item-094.body: Funded MS assistantship with Dr. Brian D. Gerber on mesocarnivore (small carnivore) ecology ��� student develops a project + sampling frame and leads field surveys. $3,000/mo stipend + tuition + fees + health. The Mar 1, 2026 deadline has passed; watch/inquire for Fall 2027.
item-094.desc: Dr. Brian D. Gerber’s lab (Colorado State University, Dept. of Fish, Wildlife & Conservation Biology) offers a funded MS assistantship in mesocarnivore ecology. The student develops a project proposal and sampling frame in year one and leads field surveys — a strong match for Erin’s mammalogy and wildlife-management coursework. Funding: $3,000/month stipend + tuition + fees + health insurance, Fall 2026–Spring 2029. The March 1, 2026 application deadline for Fall 2026 has passed, so this is flagged for the next cycle (~March 2027 for Fall 2027 start). A brief, well-targeted inquiry email to Dr. Gerber now (subject "MS mesocarnivore", with cover letter, CV with 3–4 references, and unofficial BS transcripts) establishes contact early.

item-103.title: Environmental Restoration Field Technician — wetland & habitat restoration crew
item-103.org: Riparia Environmental (MacKay Sposito subsidiary) · Fife, WA
item-103.body: Seasonal Pacific Northwest field tech role doing wetland and upland restoration — native plantings, invasive/noxious weed control (including herbicide app), and site upkeep across WA/OR/ID/CA projects.
item-103.desc: A hands-on entry-level seasonal role with a regional environmental consultancy — solid resume-builder for restoration ecology and a way to break into PNW work. Pay is $22–$26/hr depending on experience, and full-time employees get benefits (medical/dental/vision, 401k match, PTO). Labor-intensive: bending, squatting, carrying 30–40 lb loads (up to 90 occasional). Good fit for Erin's restoration ecology and wetlands interests, and a route into the West Coast region. Apply through Riparia's career page; entry-level (0–1 yr) acceptable.

item-107.title: LiDAR Monitoring Technician — USFWS Region 4 (AmeriCorps placement)
item-107.org: Stewards Individual Placement (Conservation Legacy) / USFWS · Crawfordville, FL
item-107.body: AmeriCorps individual placement with USFWS in the Florida panhandle using LiDAR data to monitor refuge habitat and inform management.
item-107.desc: A rare GIS/remote-sensing-forward corps placement — directly leverages Erin's GIS coursework and multi-criteria suitability work. AmeriCorps benefits include living stipend plus education award on completion. Southeast region (Apalachicola/St. Marks NWR area). Good resume signal for an eventual federal Pathways or grad school remote-sensing track. Apply via Conservation Legacy portal; positions fill on rolling basis so apply early.

item-109.title: Louisiana State University — Funded PhD, Conservation Ecology (Entomology)
item-109.org: Baton Rouge, LA · Department of Entomology
item-109.body: Funded PhD in LSU's Entomology Dept with a conservation-ecology focus — assistantship $30,667/yr with non-resident tuition waiver and standard benefits.
item-109.desc: Posted on the Texas A&M Natural Resources Job Board in early June 2026. Although housed in Entomology, the project is conservation-ecology framed — good for Erin if she's open to insect-pollinator or aquatic-insect angles on ecosystem health (a real growth area in EJ/restoration work). LSU's Baton Rouge programs give her another Southeast option to pair with the LSU WABL/Coastal records she's already considering. Reach out to potential advisors before applying — funding is tied to specific faculty.
item-109.badge: Reach / review — verify specific advisor + project fit before applying
